question: What was the original scope of Regulation 22 in the SEBI (Investment Advisers) R
ref_answer: The original Regulation 22 (2013) was titled 'Segregation of execution services' and applied only to investment advisers that are banks, NBFCs, and body corporates which also provide distribution or execution services. Such entities could continue offering distribution services alongside advice provided they maintained operational segregation, ensured clients were not obligated to avail distribution services, maintained arms-length relationships, and ensured fees were paid directly to service providers. Individual investment advisers were not explicitly covered. The SEBI (Investment Advisers) (Amendment) Regulations, 2020 (w.e.f. 30-09-2020) completely substituted Regulation 22 — retitling it 'Client level segregation of advisory and distribution activities.' question: How did the SEBI Takeovers Regulations provisions on combined open offer and del
ref_answer: In 2013, a basic Regulation 5A was inserted allowing an acquirer making an open offer to also delist under the Delisting Regulations, 2009, with specific disclosure conditions.
